ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002700 covers ramen noodle soup as a category. No exact code exists for spicy chicken broth ramen with multiple protein toppings; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers ramen noodle soup. No specific code for spicy variant with chashu, braised pork, and flavoured egg toppings; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific ramen dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of spicy chicken broth ramen with special toppings, approximately 600 g, comprising noodles, broth, two pieces of chicken char siew, two pieces of pork char siew, one piece of braised pork, and one flavoured egg.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen noodle soup with meat toppings — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for ramen noodle soup (FDC ID 167892), chicken chashu (FDC ID 174608), pork chashu (FDC ID 167903), braised pork belly (FDC ID 168164), and soft-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424), weighted by typical serving proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this specific restaurant dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for ramen broth noodle base (~55% by weight), chicken chashu (~12%), pork chashu (~12%), braised pork belly (~8%), and flavoured soft-boiled egg (~8%), with remaining weight attributed to broth and garnish (~5%). The composite per-100g values are derived from these weighted proportions. Sodium is elevated due to broth and seasoning. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Noodle soup GI estimate based on wheat noodles in broth — analogous to 'instant noodles, boiled' (GI 52) and 'wheat noodles, boiled' (GI 53–58) from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific spicy ramen dish. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for boiled wheat noodles (GI 52–58) and instant noodles in broth (GI 52). The protein and fat content from meat toppings and broth are expected to moderately lower the effective GI of the meal. GL per serving calculated as (55 × 58.8 g carbohydrate per 600 g serving) / 100 = 32.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content from seasoned broth and cured meat toppings
hypertension.guidance: A single 600 g serving provides approximately 2880 mg sodium, which exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-sodium broth, and avoid drinking the remaining soup.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat noodles partially offset by protein and fat from multiple meat toppings
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of approximately 32 per serving is moderate to high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller noodle portion, prioritise the protein-rich toppings, and monitor postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from pork char siew and braised pork belly, and dietary cholesterol from egg and pork
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high sodium (~2880 mg per serving), saturated fat (~11.4 g per serving), and cholesterol (~372 mg per serving) makes this dish a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease; consumption should be infrequent and portion-controlled for individuals with established CVD risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Broth Ramen with Special Toppings is a Japanese-style ramen dish served at Ramen Matsuri in Singapore. The dish features a spicy chicken-based broth as its foundation, enriched with chilli paste and seasoning, served over springy wheat ramen noodles. The special toppings configuration includes two pieces of chicken char siew (chashu), two pieces of pork char siew, one piece of braised pork belly, and one flavoured soft-boiled egg (ajitsuke tamago). The chicken char siew is typically roasted or grilled chicken thigh marinated in soy, mirin, and sake, while the pork char siew is slow-cooked pork belly or shoulder with a caramelised glaze. The braised pork is tender, slow-cooked pork belly in a soy-based braising liquid. The flavoured egg is marinated in a soy-mirin mixture, yielding a jammy yolk. Nutritionally, this is a protein-rich, moderate-carbohydrate dish with significant sodium content due to the seasoned broth and cured meats. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 118 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 5.4 g fat, and 9.8 g carbohydrate. A full 600 g serving delivers roughly 708 kcal and 49 g protein, making it a substantial meal. The high sodium content (~2880 mg per serving) is a primary clinical concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate to high due to the noodle base. This dish is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets due to the pork components.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific restaurant dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the individual components (ramen broth base, chicken chashu, pork chashu, braised pork belly, flavoured egg). GI is estimated from analogous wheat noodle entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA FoodData Central sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the approved source data for this composite estimate. Sodium value is elevated due to seasoned ramen broth and cured meat toppings, which is characteristic of restaurant ramen dishes. This dish contains pork and is not halal. The is_halal flag is set to false. Price sourced from field survey data listing for Ramen Matsuri at SGD 22.67. Serving size of 600 g is an estimate based on typical Japanese restaurant ramen bowl portions; actual weight may vary by restaurant. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle entries; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists.
